page.title: Marbella Family Boat Day with Paddleboarding and Snorkeling

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella family boat day with paddleboarding and snorkeling. From €749 for 2h on our Astondoa 40. Licensed skipper, drinks, snacks included.
data.summary: Experience a family boat day in Marbella with paddleboarding and snorkeling included. Our fleet departs Puerto Banús with licensed skippers, drinks, and snacks.
key_facts: Family-friendly boats: Astondoa 40 (9 pax), Azimut 39 (11 pax), Lagoon 380 (10 pax)
Paddleboarding and snorkeling equipment provided on all charters
Prices from €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours (Tier A boats)
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA
Departures from Puerto Banús, Marbella – easy access to Cala del Faro and Cabopino
Soft drinks, water, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks included
Tier B boats like Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h) include a jet ski for extra fun
Custom durations available – pricing on request via WhatsApp for other boats

faq.q: What boats are best for a family boat day in Marbella?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats – Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – are ideal for families. They offer spacious decks, sun pads, and easy access for paddleboarding and snorkeling. For larger groups, the Lagoon 380 catamaran (11.6m, 10 pax) provides stability and plenty of room.

Yes, we provide paddleboarding equipment on all our charters. Our crew will help you set up and offer tips for beginners. Popular spots include the calm waters of Cala del Faro and the coastline near Cabopino.

Absolutely. Snorkeling masks, fins, and snorkels are included on every charter. We recommend stopping at Cala Cortés or the rocky coves east of Puerto Banús, where visibility is excellent and marine life is abundant.

faq.q: What is included in the price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Paddleboarding and snorkeling gear are also included at no extra cost.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks. Our galley has a fridge and storage space. We also provide a selection of beverages and snacks, but feel free to customise your menu for the day.

faq.q: What are the best family-friendly spots near Marbella for snorkeling?
faq.a: Top spots include Cala del Faro (sheltered cove with clear water), Cala Cortés (rocky seabed with fish), and the coast near Cabopino. Our skipper knows the best anchorages based on wind and sea conditions.

faq.q: How long should we book for a family boat day?
faq.a: A 4-hour charter (morning or afternoon) is perfect for families with young children. For a full day of paddleboarding, snorkeling, and lunch at a beach club, an 8-hour charter offers maximum flexibility. Our Tier A boats start at €749 for 2 hours.

faq.q: Do we need to know how to paddleboard or snorkel?
faq.a: Not at all. Our crew provides basic instruction for beginners. Paddleboarding is easy to pick up, and snorkeling gear is simple to use. Children aged 5+ can usually manage with a life jacket and supervision.

data.body_html:
Spend a day on the water with your family, paddleboarding along the Costa del Sol and snorkeling in crystal-clear coves. Our Marbella family boat day with paddleboarding and snorkeling departs from Puerto Banús, with boats sized from 6.4m to 29m. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and all equipment. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on our Tier A boats, including VAT. No hidden fees, no surprises – just a day of sun, sea, and sand.

Why Choose a Family Boat Day in Marbella?

Marbella’s coastline offers sheltered coves, warm Mediterranean water, and calm seas ideal for families. From Puerto Banús you can reach Cala del Faro in 15 minutes – a quiet cove perfect for paddleboarding. Further east, Cabopino’s sandy beaches and clear water make for excellent snorkeling. With our boats, you control the itinerary: stop for a swim, explore a beach, or anchor for lunch. Our skippers know the best spots for children, ensuring safe and shallow anchorages.

Our Family-Friendly Fleet

We have boats to suit every family size. For small groups (up to 5 pax), the Mariah SX21 (6.4m) or Dubhe (8m) are nimble and easy to board. For up to 9 pax, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m) offers a spacious cockpit and sun pad. The Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) has a large bow seating area perfect for kids. For larger families or groups up to 12 pax, the Lagoon 380 catamaran (11.6m) is stable and has ample deck space. All boats depart from Puerto Banús and include paddleboarding and snorkeling gear.

Paddleboarding and Snorkeling Equipment

Every charter includes a stand-up paddleboard (SUP) and full snorkeling sets – masks, fins, and snorkels. Our crew will help you launch the paddleboard from the boat and offer basic instructions. Popular paddleboarding routes include the calm waters inside Puerto Banús harbour and the sheltered bay at Cala del Faro. For snorkeling, we recommend the rocky outcrops near Cala Cortés, where you can spot bream, octopus, and colourful wrasse. Visibility often exceeds 15 metres in summer.

What’s Included in Your Charter

All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Paddleboarding and snorkeling gear are provided at no extra cost. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours, €1,049 for 3 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, €1,549 for 5 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 and variants) start at €4,719 for 4 hours and include a jet ski. For other boats, contact us via WhatsApp for pricing.

Sample Itineraries for Families

4-Hour Morning Charter: Depart Puerto Banús at 10:00. Cruise east to Cala del Faro (15 min). Paddleboard and snorkel for 1 hour. Continue to Cabopino (20 min) for a swim stop. Return via the Golden Mile, spotting La Concha mountain. Back at 14:00.

8-Hour Full Day: Depart 10:00. Head west to Estepona (30 min). Anchor at Playa del Cristo for paddleboarding and snorkeling. Lunch at a beach club or picnic onboard. Afternoon cruise to Sotogrande (20 min) for a final swim. Return by 18:00.

Safety and Comfort for Children

All our boats carry life jackets for children (ages 2–12) and adults. Our skippers are experienced with families and will anchor in shallow, calm water. The boats have shaded areas (bimini tops or flybridge) to protect from the sun. We recommend bringing sunscreen, hats, and towels. Children must be supervised at all times – our crew is happy to assist but parents remain responsible.

Booking and Cancellation

Bookings are confirmed via WhatsApp or email. A 50% deposit secures your date, with the balance due 7 days before departure. Cancellations up to 48 hours before departure receive a full refund. Weather cancellations are free – we monitor conditions and will reschedule or refund. To book your Marbella family boat day with paddleboarding and snorkeling, get in touch today.

Frequently asked questions

What boats are best for a family boat day in Marbella?

Our Tier A boats – Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – are ideal for families. They offer spacious decks, sun pads, and easy access for paddleboarding and snorkeling. For larger groups, the Lagoon 380 catamaran (11.6m, 10 pax) provides stability and plenty of room.

Is paddleboarding equipment provided?

Yes, we provide paddleboarding equipment on all our charters. Our crew will help you set up and offer tips for beginners. Popular spots include the calm waters of Cala del Faro and the coastline near Cabopino.

Do you provide snorkeling gear?

Absolutely. Snorkeling masks, fins, and snorkels are included on every charter. We recommend stopping at Cala Cortés or the rocky coves east of Puerto Banús, where visibility is excellent and marine life is abundant.

What is included in the price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Paddleboarding and snorkeling gear are also included at no extra cost.

Can we bring our own food and drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks. Our galley has a fridge and storage space. We also provide a selection of beverages and snacks, but feel free to customise your menu for the day.

What are the best family-friendly spots near Marbella for snorkeling?

Top spots include Cala del Faro (sheltered cove with clear water), Cala Cortés (rocky seabed with fish), and the coast near Cabopino. Our skipper knows the best anchorages based on wind and sea conditions.

How long should we book for a family boat day?

A 4-hour charter (morning or afternoon) is perfect for families with young children. For a full day of paddleboarding, snorkeling, and lunch at a beach club, an 8-hour charter offers maximum flexibility. Our Tier A boats start at €749 for 2 hours.

Do we need to know how to paddleboard or snorkel?

Not at all. Our crew provides basic instruction for beginners. Paddleboarding is easy to pick up, and snorkeling gear is simple to use. Children aged 5+ can usually manage with a life jacket and supervision.
